programmes.show_description: A childcare provider and a disgruntled father both accuse each other of filing false claims of child abuse. S10, Ep125
A young man claims his ex-lover broke in, slashed his bed sheets and stole his skateboard and video game. S10, Ep126
A woman sleeps in parks before moving in with her niece whom she sues for illegal eviction and stolen property. S23, Ep89
Beautiful trees and bushes are allegedly chopped down by a tenant who insists that they were half-dead and demands money for clean-up. S23, Ep90
A woman denies any wrongdoing when a client claims she did shoddy work and damaged his business. S23, Ep91
A woman accuses her neighbour of stalking her, selling drugs and threatening her with a firearm. S23, Ep92
After a major accident, a woman sues a mechanic for failing to fix her beloved truck and for causing more damage in the process. S23, Ep93
A grandmother fights with her young neighbours over a fence that her deceased husband built 40 years ago; a video and an alleged assault come into play. S23, Ep94
The Judge is outraged that a tenant was allegedly forced to live in a place where a large dog relieved itself in her home for over a year. S23, Ep95
Punchy reality show about a female judge who has made a real impact on the American justice system. Judge Judy is real, honest and keen to get to the bottom of emotional cases. S10, Ep120
Punchy reality show about a female judge who has made a real impact on the American justice system. Judge Judy is real, honest and keen to get to the bottom of emotional cases. S10, Ep122
A woman is shot to death in her store just one day before she is to testify against the man accused of robbing her. The robber becomes the prime suspect, but he has a solid alibi. S8, Ep5
In 1993, Florida was known for attacks on tourists. An injury to a victim, a bite mark, would be a key piece of evidence used to convict a determinedly uncooperative suspect. S8, Ep6
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
The body of Jessica Knott is meticulously analysed and evidence reveals a startling truth about the killer. S7, Ep29
When a young woman vanishes, police suspect a man with a history of assault. But can they make a conviction? S7, Ep30
Investigators are flummoxed after a middle-aged woman is found dead in her home. The ex-wife of her husband could hold the key... S7, Ep31
A bloody trail leads investigators to a dead end at the scene of a bloody murder. Years later, advances in forensic techniques help find the killer. S7, Ep32
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
A gunman killed a worker at a manufacturing plant. He hoped to have covered his tracks, but forensics enabled police to track him down. S7, Ep33
Find out how police tracked down a killer using a computer, after receiving an anonymous letter about the deaths of several prostitutes. S7, Ep35
In 2019 in Hays, Kansas, Colby Trickle called 911 and said he woke up to his ears ringing and saw his wife, Kristen Trickle, bleeding next to him in the bed. S36, Ep28
In May of 2018, detective Brian Fanion dialled 911. When police arrived at the scene, they found 51-year-old Amy Fanion dead on the dining room floor. S36, Ep29
Nicki Myers Bates was just seven years old in 2006 when her mother vanished and the man her mum lived with told police he watched her die. S36, Ep30
When Margaret McOnie is found dead on the cliff tops, a nationwide manhunt followed. Her murderer would soon be revealed as a deadly conman and a double murderer. S6, Ep2
In 2014, 18-year-old UK college student Jamil Palmer was found fatally stabbed in Crane Park, Hounslow. He had suffered three stab wounds, one of which pierced his heart. S1, Ep9
In 1993, Stephen Lawrence was attacked and stabbed twice by a group whilst waiting for a bus with his friend in London, UK, in a racially motivated attack. S1, Ep10

When cousins decide to go into business together, their first delivery attempt ends with threats from customers over damaged goods. S23, Ep67
Two young boys are accused of bullying an allegedly unsupervised four-year-old and vandalising a car with a branch. S23, Ep62
The purchase of a Bengal cat by a beginner breeder ends with a fight over the pick of the litter and a cat's death. S23, Ep63
A newlywed says she had to purchase a last-minute wedding dress for $1,500 instead of $350 due to an allegedly shoddy fashion designer's work. S23, Ep64
A woman is admitted to hospital in 'deep shock' after allegedly witnessing her mother-in-law being attacked by their tax preparer's dogs. S23, Ep65
A woman sues her neighbour for the cost of a fence, harassment and damages for filing an allegedly false report with the health department. S23, Ep66
The Judge says anyone foolish enough to co-sign on a lease for a bunch of kids can't get one on their own! S23, Ep61
A well-intended mother tries to crowd too many party items into her garden; when the carnival equipment is moved to the street, alleged damages occur. S23, Ep68
A woman says she was going through a hard time, so she borrowed money from a friend for 'beauty' treatments to cheer her up. S23, Ep69
A woman discovers that her wages are being garnished to satisfy her husband's child support payments to his ex-girlfriend. S23, Ep70
In 2014, 18-year-old UK college student Jamil Palmer was found fatally stabbed in Crane Park, Hounslow. He had suffered three stab wounds, one of which pierced his heart. S1, Ep9
In 1993, Stephen Lawrence was attacked and stabbed twice by a group whilst waiting for a bus with his friend in London, UK, in a racially motivated attack. S1, Ep10
When an unreliable, drugged up prostitute witnesses an attack on a helpless girl, and an unidentified body is found, police wonder if the cases are connected. S5, Ep11
In 1990, three sex workers are murdered. A serial killer was on the loose and detectives had few clues. Over 20 years later there's a break in the case. S16, Ep11
When the wife of a reputable doctor goes missing, investigators discover the doctor house hunting with another woman claiming to be his wife. S5, Ep12
A morning house fire kills the only doctor in a small town. An investigation quickly leads to the arrest of Jason Lively, the son of the doctor's nurse. S16, Ep12
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
A woman recognised a detailed drawing of a serial rapist. Police closed in on the suspect and used forensic evidence to bring him down. S7, Ep36
When a family all develop signs of ill-health, the mother happens upon a toxic mould expert who could have the answer to their problems. S7, Ep37
A respected physician is murdered and investigators must use the knowledge of cell phone mapping and wiretapping to catch the killer. S7, Ep38
The family of a dead man claimed he had gone out walking alone and never returned. S7, Ep39
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
When a woman is convicted of killing her own mother, a new theory from an expert witness could prove to be her saving grace. S7, Ep41
Police close in on the kidnapper of teenage girls who tells them where he dumps the bodies. Can they catch him before he strikes again? S7, Ep42
In November of 2016, Dr. Eric Scott Sills called 911 and reported finding his wife and business partner unresponsive at the bottom of the stairs. S36, Ep31
One woman, two husbands dead under strange circumstances - could she be that unlucky? Correspondent Erin Moriarty investigates. S30, Ep2
When Danny Dyke tried playing the hardened drug dealer, he found himself in over his head, but it was his murderers who ended up having sleepless nights. S6, Ep3
When Michael Barber finds his wife Susan in bed with another man, she realises she has to take drastic measures to stop him leaving, but no one could suspect how far she would go. S6, Ep4
In East Sussex, UK, Lewis Ashdown murdered YouTuber Marc Williams after pressuring him to drink excessively. He filmed the entire attack and was and sentenced to life in prison. S1, Ep1
In the US, Lori Isenberg poisoned her husband but told authorities he had drowned, to cover up her fraud. She was eventually caught and pleaded guilty to second degree murder. S1, Ep2

A woman claims the father of her daughter illegally evicted her; he countersues for harassment. S23, Ep76
A young woman sues her ex-business partner for damages resulting from the dissolution of their alleged partnership in a salon. S23, Ep72
A young woman vents to a family friend about her financial troubles after being involved in a hit and run; the friend takes action but expects to be paid back. S23, Ep73
A dog's eye fills with blood after a skirmish with a neighbour's cat; the neighbour denies that her allegedly gentle, loving cat was involved. S23, Ep74
A mechanic makes the mistake of parking a client's car on the street instead of in his shop; he is sued for damages when the vehicle is stolen. S23, Ep75
When a woman sues her ex-friend for excessive mileage on a leased car, the Judge explains why it was a very bad idea. S23, Ep71
